title: Senior Teacher (EIP) - Northeast
description:
Duties & Responsibilities

  • Provide early childhood education and developmentally appropriate instruction to children with developmental delays or disabilities.
  • Create and implement individualized educational plans (IEPs) for each child based on their specific needs and goals.
  • Utilize evidence-based teaching strategies and interventions to support children’s cognitive, social, emotional, and physical development.
  • Plan and conduct engaging and inclusive lessons and activities that promote language development, motor skills, and overall learning.
  • Collaborate with a team of professionals, including therapists, psychologists, and speech therapists, to ensure a coordinated and holistic approach to the child’s care and development.
  • Use adaptive and assistive technologies, as needed, to facilitate children’s participation and access to learning opportunities.
  • Foster a nurturing and supportive learning environment that encourages children’s curiosity, exploration, and positive social interactions.
  • Work closely with parents, caregivers, and families to involve them in the child’s learning journey and provide guidance on reinforcing skills and concepts at home.
  • Assess children’s progress regularly through observations, assessments, and informal evaluations, and adjust instructional strategies accordingly.
  • Maintain accurate and confidential records of children’s progress, attendance, and behaviour.
  • Participate in team meetings and contribute to the development and review of IEPs and intervention plans.
  • Collaborate with the early intervention centre staff to support the integration of therapy goals and strategies into the classroom setting.
  • Ensure compliance with relevant educational regulations, policies, and best practices in early childhood education.
  • Attend professional development opportunities and training sessions to stay updated with the latest research and teaching methodologies.
  • Support children’s transition to other educational settings or programs, providing recommendations and follow-up services as needed.
  • Adapt instructional materials and activities to accommodate individual learning styles and developmental levels.
  • Foster a positive and inclusive classroom culture that celebrates diversity and respects each child’s unique strengths and challenges.
  • Encourage and facilitate peer interactions and cooperative play among children.
  • Provide emotional support and behavioral guidance to help children develop self-regulation and coping skills.

Requirements:

  • Possess Diploma in Early Childhood and/or Advanced Diploma in Early Childhood Intervention-Special Needs (ADESN)

  • At least 2 years of relevant working experience in the preschool environment or working with special needs children

  • Proficient in PECS, positive reinforcement and behavioral management strategies and other early intervention teaching methodology is referred

  • Proficient in crafting and planning of IEP

  • Good empathy, tolerance and temperament towards children is a must

  • Good communication, presentation and interpersonal skills

  • Able to work well independently as well as within a team

    Interested candidates, please click Apply or send your detailed resume to:

    HR@ascendrecruitment.sg

    Ascend Recruitment Pte Ltd (EA License No.: 24C2089)

    April Lim Pei Ying (Reg. No.: R22110702)

    We regret that only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.
